seoTitle: Connect Power BI to a PostgreSQL Database: Full Guide
metaDescription: Follow this full setup guide to connect Power BI to a PostgreSQL database, from configuring settings to loading and visualizing your data.
intro: PostgreSQL is a common backend for internal apps and analytics pipelines, and getting it talking to Power BI unlocks visualization without needing to export anything manually. This guide covers configuring PostgreSQL for the connection, connecting from Power BI, and loading your first dataset. It's aimed at analysts and developers setting this connection up for the first time.

steps.heading: Configure PostgreSQL for the connection
steps.body: Make sure your PostgreSQL server allows external connections and that you have the host, port, database name, and valid login credentials ready.

steps.heading: Connect Power BI to PostgreSQL
steps.body: Use Power BI Desktop's PostgreSQL connector, enter your server details, and authenticate to establish the connection.

steps.heading: Load and visualize your data
steps.body: Select the tables or write a custom SQL query to bring in exactly the data you need, then start building visuals around it.

steps.heading: Review real-world use cases
steps.body: This connection is commonly used for financial reporting, customer analytics, or operational dashboards where PostgreSQL is the system of record.

steps.heading: Optimize your connection and reports
steps.body: Use DirectQuery for large, frequently changing tables and Import mode for smaller, more stable datasets to balance performance and freshness.

faq.question: Do I need a special driver to connect Power BI to PostgreSQL?
faq.answer: Power BI Desktop includes a native PostgreSQL connector, though older versions may prompt you to install the Npgsql provider separately.

faq.question: Why is Power BI unable to reach my PostgreSQL server?
faq.answer: This is usually a network or firewall issue — confirm the server accepts remote connections and that your IP is allowed in PostgreSQL's pg_hba.conf and any firewall rules.

faq.question: Should I import data or use DirectQuery for PostgreSQL?
faq.answer: Import mode works well for smaller, relatively static datasets, while DirectQuery is better when you need reports to reflect live changes in the database.

faq.question: Can I write a custom SQL query when connecting to PostgreSQL?
faq.answer: Yes, Power BI's PostgreSQL connector lets you enter a custom SQL statement instead of just selecting tables, which is useful for pulling pre-filtered or joined data.
